tasks.title: Fix database audit release blockers
tasks.description:
Change plan from DB audit. Jeff deferred item #2 (dev/prod Supabase separation) and accepted item #6 (auto-backup behavior) as-is for now.

1. Remove tracked secret/temp files: untrack app/.env and app/supabase/.temp/*, add ignore rules, and rotate Apple app-specific password if it was pushed.
2. Sanitize logs: remove emails, profile IDs, recipient emails, and raw query/error objects from client and Edge Function console logs.
3. Tighten SQLite IPC scoping: add userId/teamId scoping for delete-shared-jumps, log-click-name, and sync_state, or explicitly document any intentional global/team scope.
4. Whitelist SQLite migrations: replace addColumnIfMissing identifier string interpolation with fixed, hardcoded migration definitions.

Do not implement until Jeff approves.

tasks.description: A turbo tax style web app for easily completing with thoroughness and speed the various SBA loan applications.  Customers are credit unions and banks and ie B2B and single end uses ie B2C.

tasks.title: Phase 1 · Finalize full requirements / spec doc
tasks.description: Phase 1 — Planning & Design. Lock in scope for Phase 1. Covers all wizard steps, user roles, data fields, integrations.

comments.author: Jeff
comments.html:
MARKDOWN SPECS:

Here is the refreshed, complete Markdown requirements and specifications document for Phase 1, incorporating the borrower document list, upload guidance, and all previous details.
# Phase 1 Requirements & Technical Specifications

## 1. Project Overview
**Product Name**: [Your Chosen Name, e.g., LoanWizard / TurboLoan / EasySBA]

**Description**: A web-based guided SBA loan application platform that makes completing 7(a) and Express applications as easy and intuitive as TurboTax. The wizard collects information step-by-step, provides smart guidance, auto-fills where possible, generates basic pro formas, validates data, handles document uploads, and prepares exports or submissions for lenders.

**Target Users**:
- Primary: Small business owners / borrowers
- Secondary: Banks & credit unions (white-label / branded instances)

**Core Value**: Reduce time, errors, and frustration in SBA applications while delivering a delightful, trustworthy experience.

## 2. Tech Stack
- **Framework**: Next.js 15 (App Router) + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
- **Hosting**: Vercel
- **Backend / Database / Auth**: Supabase (PostgreSQL, Auth, Storage, Edge Functions)
- **Emails**: Resend
- **Payments**: Stripe (Subscriptions + one-time fees)
- **Forms & Validation**: React Hook Form + Zod
- **UI**: shadcn/ui + Tailwind
- **AI Features**: Vercel AI SDK + OpenAI/Anthropic (suggestions, document insights, pro formas)
- **Analytics**: PostHog or Vercel Analytics

## 3. Key Features (Phase 1 MVP)

### Authentication & Onboarding
- Supabase Auth (email/password, magic links, optional social)
- Multi-tenant support for lender branding (subdomains or custom domains)
- Role-based access (Borrower vs. Lender Admin)

### Guided Application Wizard (TurboTax-Style)
- Multi-step stepper with progress bar, navigation, and save/resume functionality
- Auto-save every few seconds
- Conditional logic based on answers (e.g., show veteran questions or collateral sections)
- Clean, calm, professional UI with helpful explanations and tooltips

**Main Wizard Sections**:
1. Business Information
2. Ownership & Management (including veteran status)
3. Loan Request Details
4. Financial Information
5. Collateral & Additional Details (conditional)
6. Document Uploads
7. Review & Summary
8. Export / Submit

**Smart Features**:
- Real-time validation and error highlighting
- AI-assisted auto-fill and suggestions
- Basic pro forma / cash flow projection generation
- SBA program eligibility checker (including Veterans Advantage)

### Document Upload Section
Support secure uploads with clear guidance. Required/common documents include:

**Core / Always-Required**:
- SBA Form 1919 (Borrower Information Form)
- Personal Financial Statement (SBA Form 413 or equivalent)
- Business Tax Returns (last 2–3 years)
- Personal Tax Returns (last 2–3 years for owners/guarantors)
- Business Financial Statements (YTD P&L and balance sheet)
- Business Plan / Executive Summary with use of proceeds and projections
- Ownership Documents (operating agreement, articles, etc.)

**Financial & Debt**:
- Business Debt Schedule
- Aging Reports (AR/AP if applicable)
- Bank Statements (last 3–12 months)
- Cash Flow Projections / Pro Formas

**Identification & Background**:
- Photo ID for principals
- Resumes for owners/key management
- SBA Form 912 (if required by background questions)

**Collateral & Other (Conditional)**:
- Collateral Schedule and valuations
- Real estate documents (appraisals, leases, etc.)
- Business license, permits, franchise agreements, purchase agreements

**Upload Guidelines in Wizard**:
- Support PDF, JPEG, PNG, Excel/CSV
- Allow "Upload Later" with save/resume
- Provide "Why we need this" explanations and examples
- Conditional visibility based on loan type/answers
- Secure Supabase Storage with signed URLs

### Output & Lender Features
- Generate filled PDF forms and summary reports
- White-label branding for lenders
- Basic lender dashboard to view borrower applications

### Payments (Stripe)
- Lender subscriptions ($2,500/month base)
- Per-application fees ($250)
- Stripe Checkout, Customer Portal, and webhooks

## 4. Non-Functional Requirements
- **Performance**: Fast loads, responsive (mobile/tablet/desktop)
- **Security & Compliance**:
  - Encryption in transit (HTTPS) and at rest (Supabase defaults + column-level where needed)
  - Strong Row Level Security and access controls
  - Data minimization for sensitive info
  - Audit logging
  - Prepare for SOC 2 readiness
- **Accessibility**: WCAG 2.1 AA
- **Error Handling**: Friendly messages with recovery options

## 5. Design & UX Guidelines
- Style: Clean, modern, trustworthy (similar to TurboTax/Intuit)
- Tone: Helpful, encouraging, professional
- Mobile-first responsive design

## 6. Out of Scope for Phase 1
- Direct E-Tran submission (export only)
- Advanced underwriting
- Native mobile apps (Phase 2)
- Full SOC 2 audit

## 7. Success Metrics
- Wizard completion rate >60%
- High user satisfaction
- Time to complete <30–45 minutes for typical cases
- Successful pilots with early lenders

---

**Implementation Notes**:
- Prioritize the wizard UX as the core differentiator.
- Make document uploads modular and conditional.
- Build with multi-tenancy and white-labeling in mind from the start.

This spec is ready for implementation. Let me know if you want to expand any section (e.g., detailed database schema or user stories).
This version is self-contained and includes everything we’ve discussed. You can now feed it directly into your AI coding assistant. Good luck with Phase 1! Let me know what’s next.

tasks.title: Phase 1 · Create wireframes for key screens
tasks.description: Phase 1 — Planning & Design. Screens: landing, auth, dashboard, wizard steps, document upload, review, success.

tasks.title: Phase 1 · Design high-fidelity mockups (Figma) — TurboTax-style wizard UX
tasks.description: Phase 1 — Planning & Design. Focus on wizard UX — this is the core differentiator. Clean, guided, low-intimidation flow.

tasks.title: Phase 1 · Define user flows and conditional logic in the wizard
tasks.description: Phase 1 — Planning & Design. Map all branch points: loan type, business structure, ownership %, collateral, etc.

tasks.title: Phase 1 · List all form fields and document upload requirements
tasks.description: Phase 1 — Planning & Design. Full SBA form field inventory. Tag required vs optional, and conditionally required fields.

tasks.title: Phase 1 · Plan multi-tenancy / white-label approach for lenders
tasks.description: Phase 1 — Planning & Design. Define how lender branding will be applied. Subdomain vs. custom domain, branding config schema.

tasks.title: Phase 2 · Set up project: Next.js 15 + TypeScript + Tailwind on Vercel
tasks.description: Phase 2 — Development. Scaffold repo, configure Vercel project, set up CI/CD, add ESLint/Prettier.

tasks.title: Phase 2 · Integrate Supabase (Auth, Database, Storage, RLS)
tasks.description: Phase 2 — Development. Set up Supabase project. Configure auth providers, define schema, enable RLS policies.

tasks.title: Phase 2 · Implement authentication flows
tasks.description: Phase 2 — Development. Email/password + magic link. Borrower and lender roles. Protected routes.

tasks.title: Phase 2 · Build multi-step wizard with React Hook Form + Zod
tasks.description: Phase 2 — Development. Core product feature. Step-by-step SBA application wizard with validation, save progress, resume.

tasks.title: Phase 2 · Add smart features (AI suggestions, auto-fill, basic pro formas)
tasks.description: Phase 2 — Development. AI-assisted field suggestions, auto-fill from prior inputs, simple financial pro forma generation.

tasks.title: Phase 2 · Implement document upload section with guidance and conditional fields
tasks.description: Phase 2 — Development. Guided doc uploads via Supabase Storage. Show/hide fields based on loan type and borrower answers.

tasks.title: Phase 2 · Add Stripe for subscriptions and per-application payments
tasks.description: Phase 2 — Development. Subscription tiers + pay-per-application model. Webhooks for upgrade/downgrade/cancel.

tasks.title: Phase 2 · Integrate Resend for transactional email
tasks.description: Phase 2 — Development. Confirmation emails, status updates, lender notifications, password reset.

tasks.title: Phase 2 · Build lender white-label / branding system
tasks.description: Phase 2 — Development. Allow lenders to configure logo, colors, subdomain. Store branding config in DB.

tasks.title: Phase 2 · Add analytics (PostHog)
tasks.description: Phase 2 — Development. Track wizard step completion, drop-off points, feature usage. Set up funnels and session recordings.

tasks.title: Phase 2 · Implement security best practices (encryption, data minimization, logging)
tasks.description: Phase 2 — Development. Encrypt sensitive fields at rest, minimize PII stored, add audit logging for data access.

tasks.title: Phase 2 · Thorough testing (usability, edge cases, mobile, error flows)
tasks.description: Phase 2 — Development. End-to-end testing across devices and browsers. Cover all conditional branches in the wizard.

tasks.title: Phase 3 · Internal testing + bug fixing
tasks.description: Phase 3 — Testing & Polish. Systematic walkthrough of all flows. Log and fix all blockers and major bugs before external testing.

tasks.title: Phase 3 · User testing with 5–10 potential borrowers and lenders
tasks.description: Phase 3 — Testing & Polish. Recruit real users. Run moderated or unmoderated sessions. Capture friction points and confusion.

tasks.title: Phase 3 · Performance and accessibility audit
tasks.description: Phase 3 — Testing & Polish. Lighthouse audit. Fix Core Web Vitals. WCAG 2.1 AA compliance check.

tasks.title: Phase 3 · Security review (basic penetration testing)
tasks.description: Phase 3 — Testing & Polish. Run OWASP-based checklist. Test auth, RLS, file upload, and input sanitization.

tasks.title: Phase 3 · Finalize copy, tooltips, and help text
tasks.description: Phase 3 — Testing & Polish. Review every label, tooltip, error message, and help text. Plain English, no jargon.

tasks.title: Phase 3 · Set up error monitoring (Sentry or Vercel)
tasks.description: Phase 3 — Testing & Polish. Configure Sentry or Vercel error tracking. Set up alerts for critical errors in production.

tasks.title: Phase 4 · Deploy to production on Vercel
tasks.description: Phase 4 — Launch. Final production deploy. Verify environment variables, edge config, and build output.

tasks.title: Phase 4 · Set up custom domain and SSL
tasks.description: Phase 4 — Launch. Point domain to Vercel. Verify SSL cert. Set up www and apex redirects.

tasks.title: Phase 4 · Configure SEO basics (meta tags, sitemap, robots.txt)
tasks.description: Phase 4 — Launch. Add meta titles/descriptions, Open Graph tags, sitemap.xml, robots.txt, and Google Search Console.

tasks.title: Phase 4 · Create landing / marketing website pages
tasks.description: Phase 4 — Launch. Hero, features, how it works, pricing, testimonials, CTA. Optimized for conversion.

tasks.title: Phase 4 · Set up Stripe in live mode
tasks.description: Phase 4 — Launch. Switch from Stripe test mode to live. Verify webhook endpoints, pricing objects, and billing portal.

tasks.title: Phase 4 · Prepare legal docs (Privacy Policy, ToS, financial advice disclaimer)
tasks.description: Phase 4 — Launch. Draft or purchase Privacy Policy, Terms of Service, and a clear disclaimer that this is not legal/financial advice.

tasks.title: Phase 4 · Soft launch / beta with initial pilot users
tasks.description: Phase 4 — Launch. Invite 10–20 hand-picked borrowers and 1–2 lenders. Collect structured feedback before full public launch.

tasks.title: Phase 4 · Official public launch
tasks.description: Phase 4 — Launch. Announce on all channels. ProductHunt, LinkedIn, X, email list. Monitor closely for issues.

tasks.title: Phase 5 · Create marketing website content (features, how-it-works, testimonials)
tasks.description: Phase 5 — Marketing & Sales. Content-driven pages that convert visitors. Use beta feedback for early testimonials.

tasks.title: Phase 5 · Develop pitch deck for lenders
tasks.description: Phase 5 — Marketing & Sales. ROI-focused deck for small banks/credit unions. Highlight faster applications, lower dropout rates.

tasks.title: Phase 5 · Reach out to 20–50 small banks and credit unions doing SBA lending
tasks.description: Phase 5 — Marketing & Sales. Identify and contact SBA preferred lenders (SBA.gov list). Personalized outreach. Track in CRM.

tasks.title: Phase 5 · Attend fintech / banking events or webinars
tasks.description: Phase 5 — Marketing & Sales. Find relevant conferences or virtual events. Goal: meet lenders and potential borrowers.

tasks.title: Phase 5 · Content marketing — blog posts (e.g. 'SBA Application in Under 30 Min')
tasks.description: Phase 5 — Marketing & Sales. SEO-driven content targeting borrowers searching SBA loan help. Minimum 4 posts at launch.

tasks.title: Phase 5 · LinkedIn outreach and thought leadership
tasks.description: Phase 5 — Marketing & Sales. Post regularly on SBA lending, small business finance, fintech. Build credibility with lenders.

tasks.title: Phase 5 · Build email waitlist (borrowers and lenders)
tasks.description: Phase 5 — Marketing & Sales. Pre-launch waitlist + post-launch nurture sequence. Use Resend or Mailchimp.

tasks.title: Phase 5 · Prepare case studies from pilot users
tasks.description: Phase 5 — Marketing & Sales. Document 2–3 pilot stories: time saved, success rate, experience. Use for sales and content.

tasks.title: Phase 5 · Set up paid ads (LinkedIn/Google) if budget allows
tasks.description: Phase 5 — Marketing & Sales. Start small. LinkedIn for lender targeting, Google for borrower keyword intent.

tasks.title: Post-Launch · Monitor wizard drop-off points and usage
tasks.description: Post-Launch. Use PostHog funnels to identify where users abandon the wizard. Prioritize fixes for highest drop-off steps.

tasks.title: Post-Launch · Gather feedback and iterate quickly
tasks.description: Post-Launch. In-app feedback, user interviews, support tickets. 2-week sprint cycles to ship improvements.

tasks.title: Post-Launch · Plan Phase 2 (mobile apps)
tasks.description: Post-Launch. React Native or Expo app. Define scope once Phase 1 metrics are stable.

tasks.title: Post-Launch · Work toward SOC 2 compliance for larger lenders
tasks.description: Post-Launch. Research SOC 2 requirements. Identify gaps. Consider a compliance tool (Vanta, Drata) when revenue supports it.

tasks.title: Post-Launch · Track key metrics (sign-ups, completion rate, revenue, churn)
tasks.description: Post-Launch. Build a simple metrics dashboard. Weekly review of sign-ups, wizard completion %, MRR, churn.
